top_stories.title: Philippine banks keep lending rules unchanged despite war impact
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Most Philippine banks expect to keep their lending standards unchanged in the second quarter of 2026, a sign that credit growth may continue to support the economy even as the Middle East conflict weighs on the global outlook. top_stories.title: BSP directs financial firms to do cyber self-checks
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) will now require banks and other financial institutions to regularly evaluate their own cybersecurity safeguards, part of a broader push to fortify the financial system as digital services expand and online threats become more sophisticated. top_stories.title: Monetary Board raises Target RRP Rate by 25 basis points
top_stories.summary: PDate: 4/23/2026 Content: ​At its monetary policy meeting today, the Monetary Board decided to raise the BSP’s Target Reverse Repurchase (RRP) Rate by 25 basis points to 4.5 percent. secondary_signals.title: FCDU loans increased by 2.9% to US$15.56 billion in Q4 2025
secondary_signals.summary: PDate: 3/31/2026 Content: ​Foreign currency deposit unit (FCDU) loans rose to US$15.56 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025.
